narrative: Nockchain's launch of day-one Pearl merge-mining dominated the conversation (183 tracked X mentions in 24h) — a second network choosing to build on Pearl's proof-of-useful-work one month into mainnet. The bridge cleared volume in both directions, 20,178 PRL in and 7,994 PRL out over 24h, lifting custody to a new high of 766,700 PRL (~$410k). Price drifted -3.54% to $0.5354 on light $34k DEX volume after the prior snapshot's $243k burst; network hashrate eased to 28.52 EH/s. Dev cadence re-accelerated: 36 commits across four tracked repos in 7 days, led by the bridge frontend's RC5.4x series and PearlWallet v0.4.3.

market.prl_circulating_supply_method: block_height × current_block_reward (constant-subsidy approximation — Pearl is in month 1, before the first halving)
sources: pool.mineprl.com/v1/public/summary (network.height, network.block_reward_prl for circulating supply approximation)

network.active_workers_scope: MinePRL pool only — network-wide miner count is not exposed by Pearl RPC
sources: pool.mineprl.com/v1/public/summary (network.network_hashrate_hps, network.height, network.chain_nbits, economics.active_gpus, economics.network_share) — pool snapshot generated 2026-06-10T06:40Z

anomalies: Bridge cleared volume both directions over the last 24h — 20,178 PRL deposited (47 mints) and 7,994 PRL redeemed (5 burns). Custody reached a new high of 766,700 PRL; redemptions settling alongside deposits is the bridge working as designed.

/api/supply (760,898.88 WPRL) reads ~244 PRL ahead of custody totalSupplyGrains (760,654.88) across two consecutive reads — consistent with a mint settling between counters; prior occurrences cleared within the day.
Fast-lane usage reads 0% because this snapshot was taken moments after the daily 00:00 UTC window reset; the live Stats page shows intraday usage.

No 2026-06-10 snapshot was recorded (collector did not run that day); bridge deltas versus the previous snapshot span two days. Network height/hashrate reflect the pool API snapshot of 2026-06-10T06:40Z.

social.summary: The day's conversation centered on Nockchain's announcement that its AI Compute Network merge-mines Pearl from day one — debated as validation of Pearl's proof-of-useful-work versus a competitive 'vampire attack,' with GPU miners noting they earn both tokens from the same work. Separate threads speculated about a Google Cloud connection teased in the Pearl Discord, and a detailed technical exchange continued on the PoUW security paper.

sample_tweets.snippet: IT'S A $NOCK OUT! Live today. @nockchain's AI Compute Network will merge-mine Pearl from day one.
you think $NOCK doing merge mining with $PRL is a good move? buy. you don't get the vision? sell.
Taiwan, $GOOG, and $PRL… what's the connection here? seeing a Mag7 name appear (@googlecloud banner)
GPU miner here running Pearl 24/7. Merge-mining PRL + NOCK simultaneously = zero extra cost

sources: api.x.com/2/tweets/search/recent + /2/tweets/counts/recent (query: (@prlnet OR @pearlbridgexyz OR "$PRL") -is:retweet lang:en, last 24h — count includes some unrelated "PRL" acronym noise)

github.last_commit_msg: v0.4.3: fix multi-model re-audit findings C1-C8
Add nbits_override to verify_plain_proof for pool share difficulty (#161)
Potential fix for code scanning alert no. 3: Workflow does not contain permissions (#5)
